markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Add UserManual pages for parallel agent operation, backend navigation, claims, receipts, and recovery. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: ParallelSwarmUserManualPatch leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ParallelSwarmUserManualPatch is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: MT-224 adds a DB-seeded UserManual section to state-recovery-guide for parallel swarm operation and recovery. The section names live state_recovery.rs symbols for lane identity, claims, handoffs, quiet backend navigation, checkpoints, recovery receipts, indexing leases, dashboard projection, and handoff compression. It stores machine-readable runtime_symbols/negative_case_tests JSON, links existing backend-navigation-and-identity and quickstart-state-recovery pages, and cites live MT-223 negative recovery proofs instead of doc-only or mock authority.

evidence.summary: Reviewer assigned MEDIUM risk, ran independent page-link and MT-223 rollback proofs, verified no invented anchors or stale checked symbols, and confirmed the manual reads from real seeded DB content. Their non-blocking gap that try_acquire_indexing_lease was prose-only was hardened with RED/GREEN coverage and re-reviewed PASS.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-224 adds a parallel-swarm operation/recovery section to the PostgreSQL-seeded state-recovery-guide UserManual page, naming live state_recovery.rs runtime symbols and live MT-223 negative-recovery proofs, with a test that reads the page back from real Postgres and cross-checks every named symbol/test against actual source. The code implements exactly what the MT requires with real DB-backed proof; PASS.
findings: seed.rs (commit 93bb6c5e, ~lines 1183-1245): new section_with_json 'Parallel swarm operation and recovery' added to page_state_recovery_guide, carrying body_md naming 11 live symbols + 4 negative tests and body_json with runtime_symbols/negative_case_tests/authority arrays. Real change; rest of diff is rustfmt reformatting (non-load-bearing).

All 11 named runtime symbols are real pub definitions in src/swarm_orchestration/state_recovery.rs: AgentLaneIdentity (struct L180), claim_work_surface (L1340), project_swarm_dashboard (L1598), record_quiet_background_work (L2201), resolve_backend_navigation_quiet (L2286), record_role_mailbox_handoff (L2400), record_checkpoint (L2851), recover_from_checkpoint (L2932), build_handoff_compression_template (L3009), enqueue_indexing_lease (L3065), try_acquire_indexing_lease (L3114). No invented anchors.

All 4 cited negative-recovery proofs exist in tests/parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests.rs (mt223_interrupted_indexing_start_failure_leaves_no_swarm_or_kir_receipts, mt223_quiet_receipt_failure_rolls_back_index_run_and_lease, mt223_stale_indexing_lease_enqueue_does_not_leapfrog_queued_writer, mt223_restart_after_crash_reconstructs_swarm_state_from_postgres).

New test mt224_parallel_swarm_manual_patch_covers_live_runtime_symbols (user_manual_content_tests.rs L483+) uses skip_if_no_pg! + knowledge_pg() which builds a real isolated PostgreSQL schema and runs migrations (knowledge_pg_support.rs L104-145), ensure_seeded() seeds real DB, then get_page_by_slug() reads the authority row back including persisted body_json column (store.rs L57/146/497). Real Handshake-owned PostgreSQL resource touch, not a mock the implementer authored.

Code-truth binding: the test greps the actual state_recovery.rs source and parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests.rs for every named symbol/test, so manual<->runtime drift fails the test rather than rotting silently.

Both linked pages resolve: backend-navigation-and-identity is an explicit slug; quickstart-state-recovery is generated via format!("quickstart-{area}") with 'state-recovery' in the areas list (seed.rs L1392/1402/1502). No orphan/dangling page link.
sub_rule_1: no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/*Unavailable/'pending MT-'/'deferred' placeholders in seed.rs or the test on the required path.
sub_rule_3: lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260613 -> no implementer/signer collision, and proof is real regardless.
Aside (non-blocking, expected): post-commit auto-relay hook could not locate the official packet for WP-KERNEL-009 (evidence note) - paperwork/runtime-route concern only, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/src/user_manual/seed.rs (MT-224 section, page_link helper, all_slugs, quickstart_pages format! slug)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
